name: Cyberspace Operations
definition: The employment of cyberspace capabilities where the primary purpose is to achieve objectives in or through cyberspace. (JP 3-0)

name: Electromagnetic Warfare
definition: Military action involving the use of electromagnetic and directed energy to control the electromagnetic spectrum or to attack the enemy. (JP 3-85)

name: Information Operations Force
definition: A force consisting of units, staff elements, and individual military professionals who conduct or directly support the integration of information-related capabilities against adversaries. (DODD 3600.01)

name: Interpreter or Translator
definition: The capability to translate orally for parties conversing in different languages and turn documents into one's own or other language.

name: Isolated Personnel
definition: US military, DoD civilians, and contractor personnel who are separated from their unit while participating in a US sponsored military activity and must survive, evade, resist, or escape. (JP 3-50)

name: Liaison
definition: That contact or intercommunication maintained between elements of military forces or other agencies to ensure mutual understanding and unity of purpose and action. (FM 6-0)

name: Multinational Operations
definition: A collective term to describe military actions conducted by forces of two or more nations, usually undertaken within the structure of a coalition or alliance. (JP 3-16)

name: Public Affairs
definition: Communication activities with external and internal audiences. (JP 3-61)

name: Signal
definition: Provides and secures the network for commanders to conduct command and control and integrate the other warfighting functions across the range of military operations. (See FM 6-02)

name: Space Forces
definition: The space and terrestrial systems, equipment, facilities, organizations, and personnel necessary to conduct space operations. (JP 3-14)

name: Special Troops
definition: An organic unit of a modular brigade, division, corps or higher echelon responsible for planning, preparing, executing, and assessing internal support requirements. (See FM 4-0 and FM 3-96)

name: Air Defense Artillery
definition: Weapons and equipment for actively combating air targets from the ground. (JP 3-01)

name: Air and Missile Defense
definition: Direct actions taken to destroy, nullify, or reduce the effectiveness of hostile air and ballistic missile threats against friendly forces and assets. (JP 3-01)

name: Air-Naval Gunfire Liaison (ANGLICO)
definition: Provides the MAGTF commander a liaison capability to plan, coordinate, and conduct terminal control of fires in support of joint and multinational forces. (See JP 3-09)

name: Directed Energy
definition: An umbrella term covering technologies that relate to the production of a beam of concentrated electromagnetic energy or atomic or subatomic particles. (JP 3-85)

name: Field Artillery
definition: The equipment, supplies, ammunition, and personnel involved in the use of indirect fire cannon, rocket, or surface-to-surface missile launchers. (FM 3-09)

name: Missile
definition: A weapon that is self-propelled or directed by remote control, carrying conventional or nuclear explosive.

name: Missile Defense
definition: Defensive measures designed to destroy attacking enemy missiles or to nullify or reduce the effectiveness of such attack. (JP 3-01)

name: Short-Range Air Defense
definition: An ADA capability which integrates existing guns, missiles, rockets, and sensors to defend against low-altitude air threats. (See JP 3-01)

name: Intelligence Operations
definition: The tasks undertaken by military intelligence units through the intelligence disciplines to obtain information to satisfy validated requirements. (ADP 2-0)

name: Anti-Armor (Anti-Tank)
definition: Provides long range direct fires with anti-tank guided missiles (ATGMs), tube-launched, optically tracked, wire-guided (TOW) anti-tank guns, or self-propelled anti-tank guns or ATGM launchers. (See ATP 3-21.91)

name: Armor (Tracked)
definition: Provides main battle tank weapon system with 120mm smoothbore cannon and increased armor protection. (See ATP 3-90.1 and ATP 3-20.15)

name: Armored (Tracked) Cavalry
definition: Conducts reconnaissance and security with armored tracked fighting vehicles to support the brigade's awareness and knowledge in the area of operations. (See ATP 3-20.96)

name: Army Aviation / Rotary-Wing Aviation
definition: Conducts attack and air movement functions in support of ground maneuver in the area of operations or area of interest. (See FM 3-04)

name: Aviation (Rotary-Wing) Reconnaissance
definition: Conducts aerial reconnaissance and security tasks in close coordination with the brigade's cavalry squadrons. (See FM 3-04 and FM 3-98)

name: Aviation (Fixed Wing)
definition: Conducts air movement of personnel, leaders, critical supplies, equipment, and systems during the conduct of operations. (See FM 3-04)

name: Cavalry (Reconnaissance)
definition: Conducts reconnaissance and security to support friendly forces awareness and knowledge in the area of operations. (See FM 3-98 and ATP 3-20.96)

name: Combined Arms
definition: Combines the efforts of armor units and mechanized infantry units to execute tactical missions as part of a combined arms operation. (See ATP 3-90.5 and ATP 3-90.1)

name: Infantry
definition: Provides Soldiers trained, armed, and equipped to fight dismounted by means of fire and movement to destroy, defeat, capture, or repel an enemy assault. (See ATP 3-21.20)

name: Mechanized Armored (Tracked) Infantry
definition: Provides armored tracked fighting vehicles to transport and support Soldiers trained, armed, and equipped to fight dismounted by means of fire and movement. (See ATP 3-90.5 and ATP 3-90.1)

name: Mortar
definition: Organic fire support to battalions, squadrons, companies, and troops, available to a commander when other indirect fire support is not available. (See ATP 3-21.90)

name: Surveillance
definition: The systematic observation of aerospace, cyberspace, surface, or subsurface areas, places, persons, or things by visual, aural, electronic, photographic, or other means. (JP 3-0)

name: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF)
definition: Civil authority law enforcement agency in the US Department of Justice that protects communities from violent criminals, criminal organizations, the illegal use and trafficking of firearms and explosives. (See ATF.gov)

name: Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN)
definition: Recognizes vulnerabilities, identifies, and understands CBRN hazards and their consequences when they appear, and responds appropriately to protect the force. (See ADP 3-37)

name: CBRN, Explosive
definition: Recognizes components that are threats or potential hazards with adverse effects in the operational environment. (See ATP 3-37.11)

name: CBRN Reconnaissance
definition: Executes operations to obtain by visual observation or other detection methods information on the potential or actual CBRN hazards and threats in an area of operations. (See ATP 3-37.11)

name: Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA)
definition: The civil authority law enforcement agency under the US Department of Justice tasked with combating drug trafficking and distribution. (See DEA.gov)

name: Engineer
definition: Provides Soldiers with technical skills and equipment to provide freedom of action or land power by mitigating the effects of terrain. (See FM 3-34)

name: Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
definition: The civil authority domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency. (See FBI.gov)

name: Fire Department
definition: The civil authority firefighting organization that operates within a municipality, county, state, nation, or special district providing firefighting, emergency medical services, and hazmat mitigation.

name: Maneuver Enhancement
definition: Provides command and control of forces from multiple branches, especially those that conduct support area and maneuver support operations for the force. (See FM 3-81)

name: Military Police
definition: Provides law enforcement activities to control and protect populations and resources to facilitate the existence of a lawful and orderly environment. (See FM 3-39)

name: Police Department
definition: A civil authority constituted body of persons empowered and authorized by a state to enforce the law, ensure the safety, health, and possessions of citizens, and to prevent crime and civil disorder.

name: Security
definition: Measures taken by a military unit, activity, or installation to protect itself against all acts designed to, or which may, impair its effectiveness. (JP 3-10)

name: United States Marshal Service
definition: A civil authority bureau within the US Department of Justice, operating under the direction of the Attorney General, responsible for protection of judges and judicial personnel, administration of fugitive operations, and management of criminal assets.

name: United States Secret Service
definition: A federal law enforcement agency under the Department of Homeland Security charged with conducting criminal investigations and protecting US political leaders, their families, and visiting heads of state or government. (See secretservice.gov)

name: Unmanned Aircraft System
definition: That system whose components include the necessary equipment, network, and personnel to control an unmanned aircraft. (JP 3-30)

name: Aerial Delivery / Rigger
definition: Aerial delivery is the air transport of cargo, equipment, and personnel to a desired location on the ground by aircraft. (ATP 4-48) Rigger provides parachute packing, aerial delivery equipment repair, and rigging equipment and supplies for airdrop.

name: Army Field Support
definition: Integrates and synchronizes delivery of US Army Materiel Command strategic capabilities and enablers to the operational and tactical points of need in support of Army Service component commands and corps during large-scale combat operations. (See FM 4-0)

name: Ammunition
definition: Provides effective and efficient handling, storing, securing, distributing, and accounting for munitions while ensuring adequate explosives safety guidelines are met. (See ATP 4-35 and ATP 4-35.1)

name: Band
definition: Serves as a combat multiplier and plays an integral part in the sustainment of forces engaged in unified land operations. (See ATP 1-19)

name: Contracting Support
definition: The planning, coordination, and execution of contracting authority to legally bind contractors in support of military operations. (JP 4-10)

name: Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD)
definition: The process to detect, locate, access, diagnose, render safe/neutralize, recover, exploit, and dispose of explosive or improvised explosive threats. (JP 3-42)

name: Field Feeding
definition: The sustained tactical feeding to meet commander's needs as determined by the mission, enemy, terrain and weather, troops and support available, time available, and civilian considerations. (See FM 4-0 and ATP 4-41)

name: Finance Operations
definition: The execution of the joint financial management mission to provide financial advice and guidance, support the procurement process, provide pay support, and provide banking and disbursing support. (See FM 1-06)

name: Human Resources
definition: Provides operational effectiveness of the Army by anticipating, manning, and sustaining military operations. (See FM 1-0)

name: Judge Advocate General (JAG)
definition: Provides subject matter experts in the core legal disciplines and are counselors, advocates, and trusted advisors to commanders and Soldiers. (See FM 3-84)

name: Maintenance
definition: Ensures unit readiness by maintaining weapons systems and equipment in a fully mission-capable status for immediate and continuous employment in complex and highly lethal environments. (See FM 4-0 and ATP 4-33)

name: Medical
definition: Promotes, improves, conserves, or restores the behavioral and physical well-being of personnel in the Army, and as directed in other Services, agencies, and organizations. (See FM 4-02)

name: Medical Treatment Facility
definition: Any facility established for the purpose of providing medical treatment. This includes battalion aid stations, Role 2 facilities, dispensaries, clinics, and hospitals. (FM 4-02)

name: Mortuary Affairs
definition: Provides for the search, recovery, identification, preparation, and disposition of human remains of persons for whom the Services are responsible by status and executive order. (JP 4-0)

name: Ordnance
definition: Provides munitions, maintenance, and explosive ordnance disposal support to generate and maintain combat power and to provide protection to Army, joint, intergovernmental, interagency, and multinational forces. (See FM 4-0)

name: Quartermaster
definition: Provides supply and field services to enable freedom of action, extend operational reach, and prolong endurance. (See FM 4-0)

name: Religious Support
definition: Provides for the free exercise of religion and religious, moral, and ethical advisement and leadership. (See FM 1-05)

name: Shower and Laundry Operations
definition: Operations that provide shower and laundry services in direct support to the health and morale of all deployed Soldiers. (See ATP 4-42)

name: Support
definition: 1. The action of a force that aids, protects, complements, or sustains another force. 2. A unit that helps another unit in battle. 3. An element of a command that assists, protects, or supplies other forces in combat. (JP 1 VOL 2)

name: Sustainment
definition: The provision of logistics, financial management, personnel services, and health service support necessary to maintain operations until successful mission completion. (ADP 4-0)

name: Transportation
definition: A logistics function that includes movement control and associated activities to incorporate military, commercial, and multinational motor, rail, air, and water mode assets in the movement of units, personnel, equipment, and supplies. (FM 1-02.1)

name: Civil Affairs
definition: Designated Active and Reserve Component forces and units organized, trained, and equipped specifically to conduct civil affairs operations and to support civil-military operations. (JP 3-57)

name: Civil-Military Cooperation
definition: (NATO) A joint function comprising a set of capabilities integral to supporting the achievement of mission objectives and enabling NATO commands to participate effectively in a broad spectrum of civil-military interaction. (APP-6)

name: Psychological Operations
definition: A robust military influence capability. (See FM 3-53)

name: Rangers
definition: Rapidly deployable airborne light infantry organized and trained to conduct highly complex joint direct action operations in coordination with or in support of other special operations units of all Services. (JP 3-05)

name: Search and Rescue
definition: The use of aircraft, surface craft, submarines, and specialized rescue teams and equipment to search for and rescue distressed persons on land or at sea in a permissive environment. (JP 3-50)

name: SEAL Team
definition: United States Navy forces organized, trained, and equipped to conduct special operations with an emphasis on maritime, coastal, and riverine environments. (JP 3-05)

name: Special Forces
definition: United States Army forces organized, trained, and equipped to conduct special operations with an emphasis on unconventional warfare capabilities. (JP 3-05)

name: Special Operations Forces
definition: Those Active and Reserve Component forces of the Services designated by the Secretary of Defense and specifically organized, trained, and equipped to conduct and support special operations. (JP 3-05)

name: Multidomain Operations
definition: The combined arms employment of joint and Army capabilities to create and exploit relative advantages to achieve objectives, defeat enemy forces, and consolidate gains on behalf of joint force commanders. (FM 3-0)
